
Grok signals are mostly duplicates of existing README entries. arXiv RSS yields two candidates with meaningful harness-layer relevance: OpenKedge (execution-bound safety governance) and SEA-Eval (evaluation harness beyond episodic assessment). Only OpenKedge clears the 3-of-4 bar with reasonable confidence based on title/abstract signals.

## Suggestions

- [OpenKedge: Governing Agentic Mutation with Execution-Bound Safety and Evidence Chains](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08601) — Proposes a harness-layer governance framework that binds agent mutations to execution-time safety checks and auditable evidence chains — directly addressing constraint infrastructure at the scaffolding level.
  Section: Research Papers > Safety & Constraint Infrastructure | Criteria: Defines or implements the execution loop, scaffolding, or runtime environment that hosts an AI agent, Manages tool dispatch, context handling, state management, or safety constraints at the harness layer, Evaluates or benchmarks agent behavior through controlled harness-mediated interactions
